summary: Tested RBAC/sub-admin feature end-to-end. Backend pytest (12 tests) 11/12 pass; one real RBAC bypass found on GET /api/clients. Frontend: fixed missing PermGuard/Team imports in App.js (was crashing the entire app), then verified super-admin nav shows Team & Access, sub-admin creation with limited perms, sub-admin nav filtering (Dashboard/CRM/Profile only), route-guard redirects for /admin/services, /admin/team, /admin/documents, edit permissions, password reset, disable, and delete flows.

critical.issue: Uses only Depends(get_current_user) instead of require_perm('crm'). Any authenticated user (including sub-admins WITHOUT crm permission) can list all clients. Confirmed: sub-admin with permissions=['documents','dashboard'] gets 200 on GET /api/clients. All other GET CRM endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE /clients, GET/POST /quotes, /portal-token) are correctly gated. Fix: change server.py line 422 to `user: dict = Depends(require_perm('crm'))`.

ui_bugs.issue: Was missing `import { PermGuard } from '@/components/PermGuard'` and `import Team from '@/pages/admin/Team'` — routes referenced them and the entire app crashed with 'PermGuard is not defined'. Fixed during testing (added imports).

action_items: Backend: gate GET /api/clients with require_perm('crm') to prevent cross-section data leak to sub-admins without CRM access.
Verify no other GET endpoints missing permission guards (spot-check confirmed /documents, /quotes, /analytics/* are correct; /services GET is intentionally public).
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/clients missing require_perm — permission bypass allowing any authenticated user to enumerate clients.

App.js was crashing at import-time because PermGuard/Team were referenced without imports. Ship-blocking regression that main agent's own smoke test should have caught (I fixed imports so testing could proceed).

Team.jsx: KeyRound (reset-pw) and Ban (enable/disable) buttons lack data-testid. Only `edit-user-{id}` and `delete-user-{id}` have testids. Consider adding `reset-pw-btn-{id}` and `toggle-active-{id}` for cleaner tests.

get_current_user re-reads DB on every request (good — perm changes take immediate effect on next call, verified). Disabled users are correctly rejected with 403 by /auth/me and all protected endpoints on subsequent calls.

success_rate.backend: 92% (11/12 rbac tests; failure is the real /api/clients bypass bug)

test_credentials: Super admin: admin@executivedistribution.com / Executive2025!. Test sub-admin (created+deleted during test): crmtest@exd.com / SubPass123!
seed_data_creation: None persisted. Test sub-admin crmtest@exd.com was created and cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_rbac.py covers: super-admin auth/me/all-sections/self-protection, sub-admin login/allowed/denied endpoints, edit-persist, password reset, disable-blocks-access. If main agent fixes /api/clients (adds require_perm('crm')), the test_edit_permissions_persist test will pass. Frontend App.js fix (PermGuard + Team imports) is already applied.
rca of the issue: 1) /api/clients bypass: `list_clients` handler declared as `Depends(get_current_user)` instead of `Depends(require_perm('crm'))` — likely copy-paste oversight during RBAC gating pass; all other /clients handlers use require_perm. 2) App.js crash: routes were added referring to PermGuard/Team components but corresponding import statements were never added; caused ReferenceError at first render of any admin route.
